phase8-features.005: [TODO] How to use the new Settings tabs, the HostDetail Compliance tab, and the Broadcast Refresh action shipped in Phase 8 (v2.0.0.0).
phase8-features.007: [TODO] Access Groups + Registration Keys — hierarchical RBAC scoping and pre-shared agent-enrollment tokens.
phase8-features.008: [TODO] Scheduled Update Profiles — cron-driven fleet updates with security-only and staggered-rollout options.
phase8-features.009: [TODO] Package Compliance Profiles — REQUIRED / BLOCKED package policy evaluated against each host.
phase8-features.010: [TODO] Audit-log enhancements — script EXECUTE entries with stdout/stderr, plus CSV export.
phase8-features.011: [TODO] Broadcast Messaging — single-shot fan-out to every connected agent.
phase8-features.012: [TODO] Report Branding (Pro+) — org logo and header text injected into every generated report.
phase8-features.013: [TODO] Custom Report Templates (Pro+) — admin-defined column selections layered on the eight built-in reports.
phase8-features.014: [TODO] Dynamic Secrets (Pro+) — short-lived OpenBAO-backed credentials that expire automatically.
phase8-features.016: [TODO] An access group is a named scope you can hang hosts and users off. Groups can nest up to 10 levels deep — a user granted a top-level group inherits every descendant.
phase8-features.018: [TODO] Click Add Group , fill in name + optional description, optionally pick a parent.
phase8-features.019: [TODO] Use the row's edit / delete buttons to rename, reparent, or remove. Re-parenting refuses cycles and depth violations with a clear 400 error.
phase8-features.020: [TODO] Registration keys live on the same tab. A key optionally binds an enrolling host to an access group AND optionally auto-approves it (skipping the manual approval gate). The plaintext key value is shown once in the create dialog — copy it before you close the modal, since there is no recovery.
phase8-features.021: [TODO] To enroll a host with a key: paste it into the agent's registration_key config field before first start, or pass it as the --registration-key CLI argument.
phase8-features.023: [TODO] Each profile schedules an apply_updates command against a tag-filtered host set on a POSIX cron schedule.
phase8-features.024: [TODO] Open Settings → Update Profiles , click Add Profile .
phase8-features.025: [TODO] Set the cron expression. The OSS parser supports lists ( 1,15,30 ), ranges ( 9-17 ), step intervals ( */15 ), day/month names ( mon-fri ), and POSIX dom/dow OR-semantics.
phase8-features.026: [TODO] Toggle Security Updates Only to limit the agent to the security channel.
phase8-features.027: [TODO] Set Staggered Window (minutes) to spread agent dispatch over a window — useful to avoid all hosts pulling at once.
phase8-features.028: [TODO] Pick an optional tag to scope the profile (omit for the entire fleet).
phase8-features.029: [TODO] Use Trigger Now on any row to fire immediately for that profile.
phase8-features.030: [TODO] Settings → Compliance Profiles & HostDetail Compliance Tab
phase8-features.031: [TODO] Compliance profiles list packages a host MUST or MUST NOT have. Constraints can include version comparisons ( >= , != , ~= , etc.) using PEP 440 / SemVer rules with a lex fallback for non-SemVer.
phase8-features.032: [TODO] Define a profile in Settings → Compliance Profiles .
phase8-features.033: [TODO] Add constraints: pick the package, set type ( Required or Blocked ), optionally constrain the version.
phase8-features.034: [TODO] Open any host in the Hosts list, navigate to its Compliance tab, and choose between two evaluation modes: Scan — server evaluates against the cached software_package inventory. Fast. Live Scan — dispatches the constraints to the agent, which evaluates against its current installed-package state and reports back via the existing WS command channel. Slower but always-current.
phase8-features.035: [TODO] The result row shows Compliant / Non-Compliant / Pending . Expand any non-compliant row to see the per-constraint violation list.
phase8-features.037: [TODO] Click the Broadcast Refresh button on the Hosts page header to send a single refresh_inventory envelope to every connected agent. The result toast surfaces delivered_count and elapsed_ms so you can verify the <5 s SLA from the UI.
phase8-features.039: [TODO] The Pro+ reporting engine prepends an org-branding strip (logo + company name + header text) to every PDF and every HTML report.
phase8-features.041: [TODO] Type your company name and any header text (e.g. "Confidential — Internal Use" ).
phase8-features.042: [TODO] Click Upload Logo and pick a PNG, JPEG, SVG, or WEBP file (≤ 1 MB).
phase8-features.043: [TODO] Click Save . The next report you generate carries the branding.
phase8-features.045: [TODO] A template is a saved column-subset (and order) for one of the eight base report types. When you generate a report with a template applied, the renderer drops columns the template omits and reorders the rest to match.
phase8-features.046: [TODO] Open Settings → Report Templates , click Add Template .
phase8-features.047: [TODO] Pick a base report type. The available-fields panel shows the column codes the renderer recognizes for that type.
phase8-features.048: [TODO] Tick the columns you want, in the order you want them.
phase8-features.049: [TODO] Save. To apply: include ?template_id=<uuid> on a /view/... or /generate/... URL, or pick the template from the Reports page when launching a generation.
phase8-features.051: [TODO] Issue, revoke, and reconcile short-lived credentials backed by your OpenBAO / Vault deployment. The plaintext value is shown exactly once at issue time — copy it immediately. SysManage stores only the lease metadata (kind, role, TTL, expires_at) and never the secret itself.
phase8-features.052: [TODO] Open Settings → Dynamic Secrets , click Issue Lease .
phase8-features.053: [TODO] Pick a kind ( token , database , ssh ), enter the OpenBAO backend role, set TTL (60 s – 24 h).
phase8-features.054: [TODO] Copy the secret from the reveal modal. Closing the dialog is irreversible — you cannot retrieve the value again.
phase8-features.055: [TODO] Use the leases table to revoke ahead of expiry or to filter by status.
phase8-features.056: [TODO] Click Reconcile to mark any ACTIVE rows whose TTL has elapsed as EXPIRED — useful if the sweeper hasn't run.
phase8-features.058: [TODO] Every state-changing action above is logged to the audit log with the operator's user, the entity touched, and the result. Use Reports → Audit Log to view, filter, and export to CSV.
phase8-features.060: [TODO] All Phase 8 UI strings are translated into 14 languages ( ar , de , en , es , fr , hi , it , ja , ko , nl , pt , ru , zh_CN , zh_TW ). Switch the active locale from your user-profile dropdown.

phase9-stabilization.005: [TODO] What changed in v2.1.0.0. Phase 9 is a polish-and-fix release that follows the Phase 8 (v2.0.0.0) Foundation Features drop — no new endpoints, no schema migrations, focused on UX, a high-impact bug fix, and bundle / test-coverage cleanups.
phase9-stabilization.007: [TODO] Scrollable nav and action bars — the top main menu, Settings tabs, and the Hosts-page action bar now pan with prev/next arrows when the viewport is too narrow to show every item, and labels like OS Upgrades never break across two lines.
phase9-stabilization.008: [TODO] Report branding fix — Phase 8.7 introduced org branding for generated reports, but the OSS report endpoint was constructing the Pro+ generators without the ORM models reference, which silently disabled the branding lookup at render time. Reports now carry the configured logo, company name, and header text as designed.
phase9-stabilization.009: [TODO] Frontend bundle — an attempted vendor-chunk split in vite.config.ts was reverted after Playwright caught a runtime TDZ error caused by React 19 + MUI 7 internal circular imports across split chunks. Default Vite chunking is in use; the chunk-size warning limit was raised to 2500 KB to silence the build warning.
phase9-stabilization.010: [TODO] Test-coverage push — 83 new endpoint-coverage tests added for previously thin areas (diagnostics, host account management, antivirus status / defaults, firewall status, third-party repos, user preferences, reports, secrets, Graylog integration, scripts, host monitoring, OpenTelemetry, packages-operations, queue, host hostname, host Graylog).
phase9-stabilization.011: [TODO] Lint / scan clean — Pylint 10.00/10, ESLint 0 errors, SonarQube scan passes. No skipped tests in the suite.
phase9-stabilization.013: [TODO] Three places previously wrapped or hid items when the viewport got narrow:
phase9-stabilization.014: [TODO] Top main menu ( Dashboard / Hosts / Updates / OS Upgrades / ... ) — items used to wrap mid-label (e.g. OS Upgrades would split across two lines). The menu is now wrapped in a ScrollableNavList that keeps every label on one line and exposes left/right scroll arrows when overflow occurs.
phase9-stabilization.015: [TODO] Settings tabs ( Integrations / Access Groups / Update Profiles / ... ) — the MUI Tabs component is now configured with variant="scrollable" scrollButtons="auto" allowScrollButtonsMobile , matching the in-app HostDetail tab strip.
phase9-stabilization.016: [TODO] Hosts-page action bar ( Approve Selected / Reboot / Update Agent / ... ) — the bottom button row now uses ScrollableButtonBar , which forces white-space: nowrap on every MuiButton child and shows MUI-styled scroll arrows on overflow.
phase9-stabilization.017: [TODO] Behavior is identical across desktop and mobile: arrows hide when the row fits, appear with a fade when it doesn't, and the focus order skips hidden arrows so keyboard navigation stays clean.
phase9-stabilization.018: [TODO] Bug fix: Report branding now appears on generated reports
phase9-stabilization.019: [TODO] If you uploaded a logo or saved a company name under Settings → Report Branding after Phase 8.7 shipped and didn't see it on subsequent reports, this is the fix. Background:
phase9-stabilization.020: [TODO] Phase 8.7's Pro+ reporting_engine looks up the ReportBranding singleton via the ORM at render time , which requires it to receive the models module from the OSS endpoint code that constructs the generators.
phase9-stabilization.021: [TODO] The OSS endpoint code ( backend/api/reports/endpoints.py ) was instantiating HtmlReportGeneratorImpl , HostsReportGeneratorImpl , and UsersReportGeneratorImpl without passing models= , so the engine's branding lookup silently no-op'd.
phase9-stabilization.022: [TODO] v2.1.0.0 passes models=models to all three generator constructors. Both the /view/... (HTML) and /generate/... (PDF) endpoints now render with branding when a row exists in report_branding .
phase9-stabilization.023: [TODO] No action required for upgraders. Existing branding rows render automatically after upgrade. If you previously worked around the issue by re-uploading the logo, you can remove the duplicate row with the existing Delete Logo action.
phase9-stabilization.024: [TODO] Bug fix: Audit-log report used the wrong generator
phase9-stabilization.025: [TODO] A latent bug in the same endpoint module routed GET /api/reports/generate/audit-log to HostsReportGeneratorImpl.generate_audit_log_report , which doesn't exist. The route now correctly calls UsersReportGeneratorImpl.generate_audit_log_report . The HTML /view/audit-log path was unaffected.
phase9-stabilization.027: [TODO] An earlier Phase 9 attempt to split the Vite vendor bundle with manualChunks — separating React, MUI core, MUI DataGrid, MUI Charts, MUI Icons, i18next, axios, react-icons, and Emotion into independent chunks — produced a runtime TDZ error on first page load:
phase9-stabilization.028: [TODO] Both messages are symptoms of the same root cause: the React 19 + MUI 7 dependency graph contains internal circular imports between react , react-dom , react/jsx-runtime , scheduler , @emotion/react , @mui/system , and @mui/material . When those packages live in different ES-module chunks, the chunks evaluate in an order where one binding is referenced before its declaring chunk has finished evaluating, hitting the temporal-dead-zone trap.
phase9-stabilization.029: [TODO] The Playwright auth.setup.ts CI job caught this — the login page rendered as a blank <div id="root"> and the heading locator timed out. v2.1.0.0 reverts to default Vite chunking and raises chunkSizeWarningLimit to 2500 KB to silence the (cosmetic) chunk-size warning during build.
phase9-stabilization.030: [TODO] Re-attempting the split is fine, but only with a verified Playwright run proving the new shape boots a fresh browser session without TDZ errors.
phase9-stabilization.032: [TODO] The Phase 9 closeout enforces a "100% clean" bar:
phase9-stabilization.033: [TODO] Pylint — 10.00/10 across the whole backend and agent codebase.
phase9-stabilization.034: [TODO] ESLint — 0 errors, 0 warnings on the frontend ( --max-warnings 0 ).
phase9-stabilization.035: [TODO] Backend tests — 4441 pytest cases, all passing. Coverage 76% (the 80% target was aspirational; SonarQube has no hard coverage gate, and the residual gap is concentrated in startup-lifecycle and Graylog/Grafana integration paths that need a live external service to exercise meaningfully).
phase9-stabilization.036: [TODO] Agent tests — 93% coverage, exceeds the 80% Phase 9 target.
phase9-stabilization.037: [TODO] Frontend tests — Vitest passes with the new ResizeObserver polyfill in setupTests.ts (jsdom doesn't ship one and the new ScrollableNavList / ScrollableButtonBar components instantiate one inside useEffect ).
phase9-stabilization.038: [TODO] SonarQube — make sonarqube-scan reports EXECUTION SUCCESS. No new issues introduced.
phase9-stabilization.040: [TODO] None. v2.1.0.0 ships zero schema changes : no new tables, no new columns, no Alembic migrations. The upgrade is a drop-in replacement of the server binaries and frontend bundle. Agents do not need to be re-deployed.
phase9-stabilization.041: [TODO] If you are upgrading from a release older than v2.0.0.0, follow the Phase 8 operator guide first to understand the Foundation Features and any Phase 8 schema migrations they introduced.
phase9-stabilization.043: [TODO] "My report still doesn't show branding after upgrading"
phase9-stabilization.044: [TODO] Verify a branding row exists: Settings → Report Branding should show your logo / company name. If the form is empty, the singleton was never persisted — fill it in and save.
phase9-stabilization.045: [TODO] Confirm the Pro+ reporting_engine module loaded successfully. Without it, OSS deployments return 402 from /api/reports/generate/... and /api/reports/view/... ; the branding lookup is a Pro+ codepath.
phase9-stabilization.046: [TODO] Hard-refresh your browser cache on the report-viewer page — the in-page iframe can serve a cached pre-fix HTML.
phase9-stabilization.047: [TODO] Re-generate one report and inspect the audit_log row: a successful render writes an entry whose details JSON includes {"branding_applied": true} .
phase9-stabilization.048: [TODO] "My nav arrows don't appear even though items are clipped"
phase9-stabilization.049: [TODO] The arrows only render when the inner scroller's scrollWidth exceeds its clientWidth . If the nav is fitting via word-wrap (older versions), browser zoom can mask overflow — set zoom to 100% and confirm.
phase9-stabilization.050: [TODO] Verify your browser supports ResizeObserver (every supported browser does — IE11 isn't a target).
phase9-stabilization.051: [TODO] If the arrows render but never become enabled, check the browser console for CSS errors against scrollnav / ScrollableButtonBar rules.
phase9-stabilization.053: [TODO] If the build fails, the most common cause is a stale node_modules ; rm -rf frontend/node_modules && npm install .
phase9-stabilization.054: [TODO] The chunk-size warning threshold is now 2500 KB. The single index chunk lands around 2 MB — large but not pathological. Custom chunk splits are tempting but unsafe with React 19 + MUI 7 (see "Frontend bundle" section above).
phase9-stabilization.056: [TODO] All Phase 8 strings remain translated in the 14 supported locales ( ar , de , en , es , fr , hi , it , ja , ko , nl , pt , ru , zh_CN , zh_TW ). Phase 9 introduced no new operator-visible strings — every change is structural (CSS, scrollable wrappers, bundle config) or under-the-hood (test fixtures, generator constructors).
phase9-stabilization.058: [TODO] External penetration test — vendor engagement; carried over from Phase 7 / Phase 8 as a budget item, not a missed deliverable.
phase9-stabilization.059: [TODO] Backend coverage from 76% → 80% — the residual gap is concentrated in startup lifecycle and Graylog / Grafana integration code that needs a live external service in CI to exercise; addressing that requires the integration-tests harness work tracked under Phase 10 stabilization.
phase9-stabilization.060: [TODO] Pro+ Enterprise-tier modules (virtualization_engine, fleet provisioning) — Phase 10 scope.

troubleshooting.005: [TODO] Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for SysManage agents including common issues, debugging techniques, and resolution strategies.

troubleshooting.009: [TODO] Identify the Problem: Gather symptoms and error messages

troubleshooting.026: [TODO] Agent attempting to connect to server
troubleshooting.045: [TODO] Verify server configuration: # Check server hostname and port ping sysmanage.example.com telnet sysmanage.example.com 8080 # Test HTTPS connection curl -k https://sysmanage.example.com:8080/health
troubleshooting.046: [TODO] Check firewall rules: # Linux (iptables) iptables -L | grep 8080 # Linux (firewalld) firewall-cmd --list-ports # FreeBSD (pf) pfctl -sr | grep 8080 # Check if port is listening on server netstat -tlnp | grep 8080
troubleshooting.047: [TODO] Verify DNS resolution: # Test DNS resolution nslookup sysmanage.example.com dig sysmanage.example.com # Check /etc/hosts for overrides cat /etc/hosts | grep sysmanage
troubleshooting.048: [TODO] Check proxy settings: # Check environment variables echo $HTTP_PROXY echo $HTTPS_PROXY echo $NO_PROXY # Test direct connection curl --noproxy '*' https://sysmanage.example.com:8080
troubleshooting.055: [TODO] Test certificate validity: # Check certificate details openssl s_client -connect sysmanage.example.com:8080 -servername sysmanage.example.com # Verify certificate expiration echo | openssl s_client -connect sysmanage.example.com:8080 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates # Check certificate chain curl -vvI https://sysmanage.example.com:8080
troubleshooting.056: [TODO] Temporary SSL bypass (development only): # In agent configuration server: hostname: "sysmanage.example.com" port: 8080 use_https: true verify_ssl: false # ONLY for development/testing
troubleshooting.057: [TODO] Update CA certificates: # Ubuntu/Debian apt update && apt install ca-certificates update-ca-certificates # RHEL/CentOS/Fedora yum update ca-certificates # or dnf update ca-certificates # FreeBSD pkg update && pkg install ca_root_nss
troubleshooting.061: [TODO] Agent appears offline in server dashboard
troubleshooting.065: [TODO] Check agent approval status: # Log into SysManage web interface # Navigate to Hosts > Pending Approval # Approve the agent if it appears in pending list
troubleshooting.066: [TODO] Verify hostname detection: # Check system hostname hostname hostname -f # Override in agent config if needed client: hostname_override: "my-custom-hostname"
troubleshooting.067: [TODO] Clear agent database: # Stop agent systemctl stop sysmanage-agent # Remove agent database rm /path/to/agent.db # Restart agent (will re-register) systemctl start sysmanage-agent
troubleshooting.072: [TODO] System slowdown when agent is running
troubleshooting.075: [TODO] Adjust collection intervals: # Increase collection intervals collection: intervals: system_info: 600 # 10 minutes (was 5) software: 7200 # 2 hours (was 1) hardware: 3600 # 1 hour (was 30 min) network: 600 # 10 minutes (was 5)
troubleshooting.076: [TODO] Disable unnecessary collection: # Disable specific collection types collection: types: available_packages: false # Expensive operation user_accounts: false # If not needed system_metrics: false # If monitoring elsewhere
troubleshooting.077: [TODO] Monitor agent resource usage: # Monitor CPU and memory usage top -p $(pgrep sysmanage-agent) htop -p $(pgrep sysmanage-agent) # Check for memory leaks ps -o pid,ppid,cmd,%mem,%cpu --sort=-%mem | head
troubleshooting.082: [TODO] OOM (Out of Memory) killer terminating agent
troubleshooting.084: [TODO] Enable memory monitoring: # Monitor memory usage over time while true; do echo "$(date): $(ps -o pid,ppid,cmd,%mem --sort=-%mem | grep sysmanage-agent)" sleep 60 done > memory_usage.log
troubleshooting.085: [TODO] Reduce data collection scope: # Limit large data collections collection: types: available_packages: false # Large dataset hardware_info: false # If static # Reduce message queue size message_queue: max_size: 1000 cleanup_interval_minutes: 15
troubleshooting.086: [TODO] Implement memory limits: # Systemd service limits [Service] MemoryMax=256M MemoryHigh=200M # Or use cgroups directly echo "256M" > /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/sysmanage-agent/memory.limit_in_bytes
troubleshooting.094: [TODO] Verify package manager installation: # Check for package managers which apt apt-get yum dnf pkg pkg_add brew choco # Verify they're in PATH echo $PATH # Test package manager directly apt --version yum --version pkg --version
troubleshooting.095: [TODO] Check permissions: # Verify agent user can access package manager sudo -u sysmanage apt list --installed sudo -u sysmanage yum list installed # Check sudo configuration for package management sudo -l -U sysmanage
troubleshooting.096: [TODO] Configure custom package manager paths: # Custom package manager configuration package_management: custom_paths: apt: "/usr/bin/apt" brew: "/opt/homebrew/bin/brew" pkg: "/usr/local/sbin/pkg"
troubleshooting.104: [TODO] Check service status and logs: # Check service status systemctl status sysmanage-agent journalctl -u sysmanage-agent --no-pager # Check for configuration errors sysmanage-agent --config-test # Try starting manually sudo -u sysmanage sysmanage-agent --verbose
troubleshooting.105: [TODO] Verify file permissions: # Check binary permissions ls -la /usr/local/bin/sysmanage-agent # Check configuration permissions ls -la /etc/sysmanage-agent/ ls -la /var/log/sysmanage-agent/ # Fix permissions if needed chmod +x /usr/local/bin/sysmanage-agent chown -R sysmanage:sysmanage /etc/sysmanage-agent/
troubleshooting.106: [TODO] Check dependencies: # Check required libraries ldd /usr/local/bin/sysmanage-agent # Verify Python dependencies (if applicable) pip list | grep -E "(requests|websocket|pyyaml)" # Check system dependencies python3 --version systemctl --version

phase8-features.011: [TODO] List every access group (flat). Each row carries parent_id ; tree assembly is the client's job. Returns 200 with an array of {id, name, description, parent_id, created_at, updated_at} .
phase8-features.013: [TODO] Create a new group. Body: {name, description?, parent_id?} . Returns 200 on success, 404 if parent_id doesn't exist, 400 on cycle / depth violation. Tree depth is capped at 10.
phase8-features.017: [TODO] Update name / description / parent. Cycle detection re-runs against the new parent.
phase8-features.019: [TODO] Delete. Children are reparented to the deleted group's parent (or to root) per the FK ON DELETE SET NULL rule.
phase8-features.021: [TODO] List every registration key without secrets. The plaintext key is only ever returned at create time.
phase8-features.023: [TODO] Create. Body: {name, access_group_id?, auto_approve?, max_uses?, expires_at?} . Response includes the plaintext key field exactly once — it is never recoverable.
phase8-features.025: [TODO] Mark revoked. Idempotent. Existing host enrollments are not affected.
phase8-features.027: [TODO] Delete the key row. (Use revoke instead if you want to keep the audit trail.)
phase8-features.036: [TODO] REQUIRED / BLOCKED package definitions evaluated against host inventory, optionally with version constraints.
phase8-features.040: [TODO] Create. Body: {name, description?, enabled?, constraints[]} where each constraint is {package_name, package_manager?, constraint_type, version_op?, version?} . constraint_type is REQUIRED or BLOCKED ; version_op is one of = , == , >= , <= , > , < , != , ~= .
phase8-features.044: [TODO] Update. Constraint list is replaced atomically — pass the full desired list.
phase8-features.048: [TODO] Evaluate the host's cached software_package inventory against the profile, persist the result in HostPackageComplianceStatus , and return it. Fast but only as fresh as the most recent inventory upload.
phase8-features.050: [TODO] Ask the agent to evaluate compliance against its live local package state and report back via the WS command-result channel. Slower round-trip but always-current.

agent-deployment-protocol.005: [TODO] Server-to-agent message protocol for the three open-source deployment primitives every Pro+ engine builds on: deploy_files , execute_command_sequence , and service_control .
agent-deployment-protocol.007: [TODO] The architectural decision made in Phase 3 split the deployment pipeline in two:
agent-deployment-protocol.008: [TODO] Server side (Pro+ Cython modules) generates fully-baked configuration — firewall rule sets, AV deployment plans, OTEL collector configs, VM definitions. This is where the proprietary value lives.
agent-deployment-protocol.009: [TODO] Agent side (open-source) just deploys files, runs commands, and controls services. It has no knowledge of what any particular file means.
agent-deployment-protocol.010: [TODO] The three messages documented here are the entire interface between the two halves. They are deliberately generic — adding a new Pro+ feature does not require any agent code change.
agent-deployment-protocol.011: [TODO] Agent implementation lives in sysmanage-agent/src/sysmanage_agent/operations/generic_deployment.py (deploy and command sequencing) and sysmanage-agent/src/sysmanage_agent/core/agent_utils.py (service control). All three return a structured result dict; none of them ever raise out of the handler.
agent-deployment-protocol.013: [TODO] Atomic file deployment. Writes one or more files to the local filesystem using the temp-file-plus-rename pattern, with optional SHA-256 verification and backup/rollback support.
agent-deployment-protocol.023: [TODO] Absolute destination path. Parent dirs are created (mode 0755) if missing.
agent-deployment-protocol.027: [TODO] Full file contents. The agent appends a trailing newline if the content does not end in one (POSIX text-file convention).
agent-deployment-protocol.043: [TODO] If supplied, the agent verifies BOTH the pre-write content (to catch transport corruption) AND the on-disk file post-rename (to catch filesystem-level mutation). Mismatch triggers rollback if a backup exists, otherwise removes the bad file.
agent-deployment-protocol.047: [TODO] Default false . When true, an existing target is copied to <path>.sysmanage.bak before the new content is written. The backup is left in place on success (admin can clean up); on post-write hash mismatch the backup is restored.
agent-deployment-protocol.049: [TODO] success is true if at least one file was deployed. Per-file outcomes are in deployed_files (each carries its own success ) and errors .
agent-deployment-protocol.051: [TODO] Run a sequence of steps in order. Stops at the first failed step. A "step" is a discriminated union of three types: shell (run a command), deploy_file (one-off file deployment, same semantics as deploy_files ), and wait_condition (poll a command's output until it matches a regex).
agent-deployment-protocol.061: [TODO] Runs via subprocess. Step succeeds iff exit code is 0.
agent-deployment-protocol.064: [TODO] same optional fields as in deploy_files
agent-deployment-protocol.065: [TODO] Single-file atomic deploy with the same SHA-256 + backup semantics.
agent-deployment-protocol.069: [TODO] Re-runs command on the interval until its stdout matches the match regex, or times out. Useful for waiting on services to become ready.
agent-deployment-protocol.071: [TODO] If progress_message_type is supplied, the agent emits one progress message per step before executing it. The message envelope uses the supplied type so callers can route progress without colliding with other in-flight commands. Default type is command_sequence_progress .
agent-deployment-protocol.073: [TODO] If a step fails, the sequence stops; completed_steps reflects the count of successful steps and errors contains the failure message. The remaining steps are not attempted.
agent-deployment-protocol.075: [TODO] Start, stop, restart, enable, or disable one or more services. The agent picks the appropriate service manager based on the host platform — systemctl on most modern Linux distros, rc-service + rc-update on Alpine/Gentoo (OpenRC), launchctl on macOS, or sc.exe on Windows. The same message works across distros without the caller knowing which manager runs the host.
agent-deployment-protocol.081: [TODO] Bring the service up now. No-op if already running.
agent-deployment-protocol.083: [TODO] Take the service down now. No-op if already stopped.
agent-deployment-protocol.087: [TODO] Mark the service to start on boot. Does NOT start it now.
agent-deployment-protocol.089: [TODO] Mark the service to NOT start on boot. Does NOT stop it now.
agent-deployment-protocol.091: [TODO] The handler refuses to operate when the agent is not running in privileged mode ( is_running_privileged() returns false). In unprivileged mode the response is {"success": false, "error": "Service control requires privileged mode"} — the caller is expected to surface that to the operator and request elevated re-deployment.
agent-deployment-protocol.093: [TODO] success is true iff EVERY service in the list completed successfully. Per-service outcomes are in results .
agent-deployment-protocol.095: [TODO] These three messages are the open-source contract every Pro+ release ships against. Backwards-incompatible changes to any of the request or response shapes documented above require:
agent-deployment-protocol.096: [TODO] A new command_type string (e.g., deploy_files_v2 ) — never an in-place schema change.
agent-deployment-protocol.097: [TODO] A migration path documented in the agent release notes.
agent-deployment-protocol.098: [TODO] A version-detection probe so older Pro+ engines can fall back to the previous wire format.
agent-deployment-protocol.099: [TODO] Adding optional new fields to existing messages is safe and does not require versioning, provided the agent treats unknown fields as no-ops.
agent-deployment-protocol.103: [TODO] Pro+ callers: av_management_engine , firewall_orchestration_engine , and container_engine all generate plans that ultimately call these three messages.

communication-protocols.005: [TODO] Advanced communication infrastructure enabling secure, real-time, and reliable data exchange
communication-protocols.011: [TODO] SysManage implements a multi-layer communication architecture designed for reliability, security, and performance.
communication-protocols.012: [TODO] Mutual TLS Encryption: All communications are secured using TLS 1.3 with mutual authentication. Both server and agents present X.509 certificates for verification, ensuring secure identity verification and encrypted data transmission.
communication-protocols.013: [TODO] Dual Protocol Support: WebSocket connections for real-time bidirectional communication and HTTP/REST for request-response operations. WebSocket provides persistent connections for immediate command execution and status updates.

communication-protocols.020: [TODO] Intelligent reconnection logic with exponential backoff, connection health monitoring, and seamless failover.

design-principles.005: [TODO] Core design principles, architectural patterns, and engineering decisions that guide SysManage development.
design-principles.007: [TODO] SysManage is built on a foundation of proven software engineering principles that prioritize security, scalability, maintainability, and operational excellence. Every architectural decision is guided by real-world operational requirements and enterprise-grade reliability standards.
design-principles.009: [TODO] Security is not an afterthought but a fundamental design constraint that influences every component and interaction.
design-principles.011: [TODO] Architecture anticipates growth from small deployments to enterprise-scale, with clear scaling paths at every layer.
design-principles.013: [TODO] Every feature considers the operational burden on administrators, prioritizing automation and self-healing capabilities.
design-principles.015: [TODO] Cross-platform design ensures consistent functionality across diverse operating systems and environments.
design-principles.018: [TODO] SysManage operates under the assumption that no component, network, or user can be inherently trusted.
design-principles.020: [TODO] Mutual TLS (mTLS): Every agent-server communication is authenticated and encrypted using client certificates
design-principles.021: [TODO] Certificate Rotation: Automatic certificate renewal with configurable expiration periods
design-principles.022: [TODO] Least Privilege: Role-based access control with granular permissions and time-limited tokens
design-principles.023: [TODO] Defense in Depth: Multiple security layers from network to application to data
design-principles.026: [TODO] Performance Impact: Encryption overhead vs. security assurance
design-principles.027: [TODO] Complexity: Certificate management complexity vs. authentication strength
design-principles.028: [TODO] Usability: Security barriers vs. ease of deployment
design-principles.030: [TODO] System components communicate through events and message queues, enabling loose coupling and asynchronous processing.
design-principles.033: [TODO] Scalability: Independent scaling of event producers and consumers
design-principles.034: [TODO] Resilience: Automatic retry and dead letter handling for failed events
design-principles.035: [TODO] Observability: Complete audit trail of all system events
design-principles.036: [TODO] Flexibility: Easy addition of new event handlers without code changes
design-principles.051: [TODO] Configuration and state changes are treated as immutable events with full audit trails and rollback capabilities.

design-principles.054: [TODO] Audit Trails: Every change recorded with who, what, when, and why

design-principles.056: [TODO] Validation Gates: Automated testing before configuration deployment

performance-stability.005: [TODO] Enterprise-grade performance engineering with comprehensive monitoring, optimization, and reliability measures
performance-stability.012: [TODO] SysManage implements multiple layers of stability measures ensuring reliable operation in enterprise environments.
performance-stability.013: [TODO] Fault Isolation: Automatic circuit breakers prevent cascade failures by temporarily blocking operations to failed services. Includes configurable thresholds, failure detection, and automatic recovery mechanisms.
performance-stability.014: [TODO] Resilient Communication: Intelligent retry mechanisms with exponential backoff, jitter, and maximum attempt limits. Handles transient failures while preventing system overload during recovery.
performance-stability.015: [TODO] Resource Protection: Advanced rate limiting protects against abuse and ensures fair resource allocation. Implements token bucket algorithms with per-user and global limits.
performance-stability.016: [TODO] Service Continuity: System continues operating with reduced functionality during partial failures. Critical operations remain available while non-essential features degrade gracefully.
performance-stability.017: [TODO] Resource Efficiency: Optimized database and network connection pooling prevents resource exhaustion and improves performance under load. Includes connection health monitoring and automatic pool resizing.
performance-stability.018: [TODO] Proactive Detection: Comprehensive health checks monitor all system components with automated alerting. Includes dependency checks, performance baselines, and predictive failure detection.
performance-stability.020: [TODO] Non-blocking I/O operations with FastAPI async/await patterns, ensuring maximum throughput and resource utilization.
performance-stability.021: [TODO] Multi-layer caching strategy with Redis for session data, application-level caching, and database query result caching.
performance-stability.022: [TODO] Persistent WebSocket connections, HTTP/2 support, and optimized connection pooling for minimal latency.

system-architecture.005: [TODO] Modern distributed architecture designed for scalability, reliability, and enterprise-grade performance
system-architecture.011: [TODO] SysManage employs a layered architecture pattern ensuring separation of concerns, maintainability, and scalability.
system-architecture.012: [TODO] React TypeScript Frontend: Modern single-page application with real-time WebSocket integration, responsive design, and comprehensive system management interfaces. Features include host management, package operations, script execution, and system monitoring dashboards.
system-architecture.013: [TODO] FastAPI REST Endpoints: High-performance API layer with automatic OpenAPI documentation, request validation, authentication middleware, and comprehensive error handling. Supports both synchronous REST operations and asynchronous WebSocket communications.
system-architecture.014: [TODO] Service Components: Core business logic handling host management, package operations, script execution, message queuing, and system orchestration. Implements complex workflows like package dependency resolution, batch operations, and state management.
system-architecture.015: [TODO] PostgreSQL Database: Enterprise-grade relational database with advanced features including JSON columns, full-text search, complex indexing strategies, and ACID compliance. Handles user data, host information, package inventories, and audit logs.
system-architecture.016: [TODO] WebSocket + mTLS Protocol: Real-time bidirectional communication with agents using WebSocket over mutual TLS. Implements persistent message queuing, automatic retry mechanisms, and connection health monitoring for reliable distributed operations.
system-architecture.017: [TODO] Cross-Platform Agents: Lightweight Python agents running on managed hosts, supporting Windows, Linux, and macOS. Handle local operations, system monitoring, package management, and secure communication with the server.
system-architecture.028: [TODO] Modular design with clearly defined service boundaries, enabling independent development, testing, and deployment of system components.
system-architecture.029: [TODO] Optimized for speed with async operations, connection pooling, efficient database queries, and minimal resource footprint on managed hosts.
system-architecture.030: [TODO] Multiple security layers including mTLS encryption, JWT authentication, role-based access control, input validation, and comprehensive audit logging.
system-architecture.031: [TODO] Designed to scale horizontally with load balancing support, stateless services, efficient caching, and distributed architecture patterns.
system-architecture.032: [TODO] Resilient design with graceful degradation, automatic retry mechanisms, circuit breakers, and comprehensive error handling and recovery.
system-architecture.033: [TODO] Clean code architecture with comprehensive testing, detailed documentation, consistent coding standards, and automated CI/CD pipelines.

mtls.005: [TODO] Comprehensive guide to SysManage's mutual TLS implementation, certificate management, PKI infrastructure, auto-rotation, and secure agent communication.
mtls.007: [TODO] SysManage implements mutual TLS (mTLS) for secure agent-to-server communication. This provides strong authentication, encryption in transit, and protection against various attack vectors including man-in-the-middle attacks, agent spoofing, and unauthorized access.
mtls.011: [TODO] SysManage implements a two-tier PKI with offline root CA and online intermediate CA for enhanced security.
mtls.016: [TODO] High Security: Hardware Security Module (HSM) protected
mtls.017: [TODO] Limited Use: Only signs intermediate CA certificates
mtls.018: [TODO] Key Length: 4096-bit RSA or P-384 ECC
mtls.020: [TODO] Online Operation: Integrated with SysManage server
mtls.022: [TODO] Automated Signing: Issues agent and server certificates
mtls.023: [TODO] CRL Management: Maintains certificate revocation lists
mtls.024: [TODO] Key Length: 2048-bit RSA or P-256 ECC
mtls.026: [TODO] Agent Certificates: Unique per agent with hostname/UUID
mtls.028: [TODO] Short Validity: 90 days with automatic rotation
mtls.030: [TODO] Key Usage: Digital signature, key encipherment
mtls.035: [TODO] Agent Registration: Agent submits registration request with host information
mtls.036: [TODO] Administrator Approval: Manual approval process for new agents

reporting.005: [TODO] How to responsibly report security vulnerabilities and issues in SysManage.
reporting.007: [TODO] We take security seriously. If you discover a security vulnerability, please follow our responsible disclosure process to help us address it quickly and safely.
reporting.010: [TODO] Send an email to security@sysmanage.org with details about the vulnerability. Include:
reporting.016: [TODO] We will acknowledge receipt of your report within 48 hours and provide an initial assessment within 7 days .
reporting.018: [TODO] Our security team will investigate the issue and work on a fix. We may contact you for additional information or clarification.
reporting.020: [TODO] Once fixed, we will notify you and coordinate the public disclosure timeline.
reporting.040: [TODO] Please do not report the following as security vulnerabilities:
reporting.041: [TODO] Issues requiring physical access to the system
reporting.043: [TODO] Missing best practices without security impact
reporting.044: [TODO] Issues in third-party software (report to respective vendors)
reporting.046: [TODO] Brute force attacks without demonstrable impact
reporting.052: [TODO] Keep the vulnerability confidential until resolved
reporting.065: [TODO] For sensitive reports, use our PGP key:
reporting.071: [TODO] We appreciate security researchers who help improve SysManage's security. Responsible reporters will be:
reporting.072: [TODO] Acknowledged in our security advisories (with permission)
reporting.073: [TODO] Listed in our Hall of Fame

installation.001: [TODO] Python 3.12+: Download from python.org ✅ Python 3.12, 3.13, or 3.14 recommended ✅ Check "Add Python to PATH" during installation
installation.004: [TODO] Git for Windows: Download from git-scm.com
installation.005: [TODO] Visual Studio Build Tools: Download from Microsoft
installation.008: [TODO] Before starting installation, NetBSD users MUST:
installation.009: [TODO] Install ALL required packages including jpeg (not just libjpeg-turbo)
installation.010: [TODO] Set environment variables: export CFLAGS="-I/usr/pkg/include" LDFLAGS="-L/usr/pkg/lib -Wl,-R/usr/pkg/lib"
installation.011: [TODO] Use gmake instead of make for all make commands
installation.012: [TODO] Manually create PostgreSQL user if not present
installation.013: [TODO] Increase /tmp size if needed: mount -t tmpfs -o -s=4g tmpfs /tmp
installation.014: [TODO] See the NetBSD tab in Prerequisites above for complete setup instructions.
installation.016: [TODO] PostgreSQL user missing: NetBSD may not create the postgres/pgsql user automatically. Create it with: sudo useradd -m -d /var/db/postgresql -s /bin/sh postgres
installation.017: [TODO] PostgreSQL won't start: Ensure pgsql=YES is in /etc/rc.conf, then run sudo /etc/rc.d/pgsql start
installation.018: [TODO] No space left on device during pip install: NetBSD's /tmp is limited to 1GB by default. Increase with: mount -t tmpfs -o -s=4g tmpfs /tmp or use export TMPDIR=$HOME/tmp
installation.019: [TODO] gmake install-dev for development: Use gmake instead of make . The Makefile includes NetBSD-specific C tracer dependency checks (gcc13, py312-cffi)
installation.020: [TODO] PostgreSQL socket not found: NetBSD PostgreSQL often uses TCP instead of Unix sockets. Always use -h localhost when connecting
installation.021: [TODO] OpenTelemetry Collector build failures: NetBSD uses BSD versions of find/xargs/dirname which are incompatible with the OTEL Collector Makefile. Install GNU utilities: sudo pkgin install findutils coreutils The install-telemetry.py script will automatically detect and use gfind/gxargs/gdirname on NetBSD.
installation.022: [TODO] Browser/WebDriver errors: Install browsers for UI testing: # Install Chromium (includes ChromeDriver) doas pkgin install chromium # Install Firefox (requires version-specific name) doas pkgin install firefox-138.0.4 # Install GeckoDriver for Firefox UI testing doas pkgin install geckodriver-0.36.0 # Verify installations which chromium firefox geckodriver Note: NetBSD Firefox packages often require explicit version numbers unlike other BSD systems. Due to Firefox WebDriver compatibility issues on NetBSD, the UI test suite automatically excludes Firefox tests and runs Chrome-only tests. This ensures clean test results without failures or skipped tests.
installation.023: [TODO] Pillow build failures: Install image libraries and set paths: pkgin install jpeg libjpeg-turbo tiff png freetype2 lcms2 libwebp openjpeg zlib export CFLAGS="-I/usr/pkg/include" export LDFLAGS="-L/usr/pkg/lib -Wl,-R/usr/pkg/lib"
installation.024: [TODO] Backend fails to start with "libjpeg.so.9 not found": This occurs when Pillow was installed before the JPEG library or with incorrect paths. The gmake install-dev target automatically rebuilds Pillow for NetBSD. For manual fix: . .venv/bin/activate export CFLAGS="-I/usr/pkg/include" export LDFLAGS="-L/usr/pkg/lib -Wl,-R/usr/pkg/lib" pip uninstall -y Pillow pip install --no-binary=:all: Pillow
installation.025: [TODO] OpenBAO installation: NetBSD doesn't have pre-built OpenBAO packages. Options: Build from source (recommended): pkgin install go git gmake # NetBSD installs Go as go124 - create symlink: sudo ln -sf /usr/pkg/bin/go124 /usr/pkg/bin/go # Add ~/.local/bin to PATH: echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.profile . ~/.profile # Then run: gmake install-dev # It will prompt to build OpenBAO from source Use Linux emulation: Try running Linux binaries under NetBSD's Linux compatibility Skip OpenBAO: It's optional - set vault.enabled=false in config Note: OpenBAO is optional for development. The system works perfectly without it.
installation.026: [TODO] OpenBAO 'bao' command not found: Ensure ~/.local/bin is in your PATH: echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.profile && . ~/.profile

welcome.description: SysManage — это комплексная платформа для мониторинга и автоматизации инфраструктуры, которая помогает вам управлять хостами, пакетами и состоянием системы во всей вашей инфраструктуре. Независимо от того, управляете ли вы несколькими серверами или тысячами хостов, SysManage предоставляет инструменты, необходимые для эффективного обслуживания и мониторинга ваших систем.
welcome.title: Добро пожаловать в SysManage

hero.subtitle: Централизованный мониторинг, управление и автоматизация для всей вашей инфраструктуры. Поддержка Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD и OpenBSD.
hero.title: Современное Управление Системами Упрощено

migration_compat_banner.action_step2: Из каталога проекта запустите alembic upgrade head (или эквивалентную цель Make, используемую вашей установкой).
migration_compat_banner.action_step4: Баннер исчезает, как только затронутые модули успешно перезагружаются на новую ревизию схемы.
migration_compat_banner.appearance: Каждый затронутый модуль перечислен отдельно с требуемой ревизией alembic и (если строка была доступна для чтения) текущей ревизией базы данных. Модули, не прошедшие эту проверку, не загружаются; соответствующие функции UI остаются недоступными, пока миграции не догонят.
migration_compat_banner.description: SysManage отображает жёлтый баннер в верхней части интерфейса, когда один или несколько модулей Pro+ не могут быть загружены, потому что схема базы данных старше, чем требует модуль. Баннер — резервная страховочная сетка: обычная процедура обновления запускает alembic upgrade head как часть установки нового релиза, и баннер срабатывает только тогда, когда этот шаг был пропущен.
migration_compat_banner.endpoint: Те же данные доступны по GET /api/license/module-compat; непустой массив incompatibilities указывает на ожидающие миграции. Системы мониторинга и предполётные проверки должны опрашивать этот эндпоинт для обнаружения дрейфа схемы до того, как пользователи увидят баннер.
migration_compat_banner.title: Баннер миграции базы данных

backend.item3: Сканирование безопасности: Интегрированные проверки бандита и безопасности
